opinion.h: NEILL ET AL. v. COOK ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF TEXAS. No. 691. Decided February 24, 1964.
opinion.p: The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
376 U.S. 202 DOUGHTY v. MAXWELL, WARDEN. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE SUPREME COURT OF OHIO. No. 422, Misc. Decided February 24, 1964.
William B. Saxbe, Attorney General of Ohio, and William C. Baird, Assistant Attorney General, for respondent.
The motion for leave to proceed in forma pauperis and the petition for writ of certiorari are granted. The judgment is reversed. Carnley v. Cochran, 369 U.S. 506, Gideon v. Wainwright, 372 U.S. 335.
